articles.headline: Kane eyes Lewandowski record: Tracking Europe's major goal, assist records
articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.name: Harry Kane [1296x729]
images.caption: Kane scored his 20th Bundesliga goal of the season today after just 16 games.
images.credit: Photo by Tom Weller/picture alliance via Getty Images

images.name: Manchester United's Bruno Fernandes [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruno Fernandes' assist tally took a sizeable leap after Ruben Amorim was sacked as Manchester United manager.

images.name: Lamine Yamal, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Neither Kylian Mbappé nor Lamine Yamal are likely to break Lionel Messi's single-season goal and assist marks in LaLiga.

images.name: Inter Milan's Lautaro Martinez [1296x729]
images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

images.name: Mason Greenwood aug 2025 [1296x729]
images.caption: Mason Greenwood was made to share the Ligue 1 top scorer title last season.

images.name: Vinicus Junior, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé is within reach of Cristiano Ronaldo's single-season Champions League goal record.
images.credit: David S. Bustamante/Real Madrid via Getty Images

images.name: Bayern Munich holds on for 3-2 win
images.caption: Harry Kane scores a brace as Bayern Munich beat Eintracht Frankfurt 3-2.

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Man United keeping tabs on Stuttgart's Andrés
articles.description: Manchester United could look to the Bundesliga for their newest midfield signing. Transfer Talk has the latest.

images.name: Chema Andrés [1296x729]
images.credit: (Photo by Harry Langer/picture alliance via Getty Images)

images.name: Is Lamine Yamal the best player in the world?
images.caption: The 'ESPN FC' crew react to Lamine Yamal becoming the youngest player to score a LaLiga hat-trick this century as Barcelona beat Villarreal 4-1.

articles.headline: Arsenal win ugly vs. Chelsea; Bayern close in on Bundesliga title; more
articles.description: Arsenal muscled past Chelsea on Sunday, but coach Mikel Arteta can't be happy at how difficult things look for his team. Also: Bayern Munich likely wrapped up the Bundesliga with a Der Klassiker win.

images.name: Arsenal celeb v Chelsea 260302 [1296x729]
images.credit: Stuart MacFarlane/Arsenal FC via Getty Images

images.name: Can Arsenal ride set-pieces all the way to the title?
images.caption: Shaka Hislop and Steve Nicol wonder if Arsenal are too reliant on set-pieces to win the Premier League title.

article.headline: Bayern end season in third place after loss at Hoffenheim
article.description: Bayern Munich slumped to a 4-2 defeat at Hoffenheim in the Bundesliga finale on Saturday, ending a disappointing season in third place.

article.story:
Bayern Munich squandered a two-goal lead to slump to a 4-2 loss at Hoffenheim courtesy of an Andrej Kramaric hat-trick in the Bundesliga finale on Saturday, ending a disappointing season in third place as head coach Thomas Tuchel bowed out.

Saturday's performance was indicative of Bayern's first season without a trophy in over a decade, having played outstandingly to reach the Champions League semi-finals, but also falling prey to weaker opponents, including third-tier Saarbruecken who beat them in the German Cup.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

Tuchel said that he was unhappy with his team's lack of focus in his final match in charge.

"We started really well but then we had a string of string of individual mistakes," Tuchel said in a short news conference.

The 50-year-old is leaving Bayern after just over a year in charge.

Early goals from Mathys Tel and Alphonso Davies put an injury-depleted Bayern 2-0 up after six minutes.

They were without leading striker Harry Kane, back in England to treat an injury ahead of Euro 2024, as well as Serge Gnabry, Kingsley Coman, Jamal Musiala and Leroy Sané.

Hoffenheim did not give up and pulled a goal back through Germany international Maximilian Beier two minutes later and the hosts gradually took some of the early pace out of the game.

"In the second half we played well for 15 minutes but then it was again a series of individual mistakes and loss of possession," Tuchel said. "We gave away a two-goal lead for a completely unnecessary and bitter defeat."

Croatia international Kramaric delivered a stunning 19-minute hat trick with the Bayern defence in ruins, to send them down to third place in the standings on 72 points.

"It happens far to often, like that. It happened in Stuttgart where we conceded two late goals [for a 3-1 defeat]. In Heidenheim the same [for a 3-2 loss], here the same. It just happens too often," Tuchel said.

"I have an explanation for that but it is not for the public."

- Transfer Talk: Bayern looking at Fernandes, De Jong
- Hosts Germany omit Hummels, Goretzka from Euro 2024 squad
- Bayern Munich back in red as 2024-25 home kit is unveiled

Tuchel was told in February he would be leaving at the end of the season, a year before the end of his contract. He confirmed there had been talks last week to potentially reverse that decision, but no agreement was reached.

Bayern are struggling to find his successor after several other coaches, including Bayer Leverkusen's Xabi Alonso, Austria manager Ralf Rangnick and Germany boss Julian Nagelsmann, opted to remain in their jobs.

Bayer Leverkusen claimed top spot with 90 points. Stuttgart finished second with 73 points with a 4-0 demolition of Borussia Monchengladbach, while Bayern ended up third on 72 points.
